data.title: BKV Files 8K - Regulation FD >BKV
data.description:
BKV Corp. (BKV) filed a Form 8K - Regulation FD Disclosure - with the U.S Securities and Exchange Commission on January 29, 2026.

As previously disclosed, on October 29, 2025, BKV Corporation ("BKV") entered into a Membership Interest Purchase Agreement (the "Purchase Agreement") with Banpu Power US Corporation ("BPPUS"), a wholly owned subsidiary of Banpu Power Public Company Limited ("BPP"), to acquire one-half of the limited liability company interests of BKV-BPP Power, LLC, a Delaware limited liability company ("BKV-BPP Power"), currently held by BPPUS upon the terms and subject to the conditions of the Purchase Agreement (such purchase and sale, together with the other transactions contemplated by the Purchase Agreement, the "Transaction").

On January 29, 2026, BPP convened an extraordinary general meeting of the shareholders of BPP to, among other matters, approve the Transaction (the "BPP EGM"). Following the conclusion of the BPP EGM, BPP announced that at least 75% of the disinterested shareholders of BPP attending the BPP EGM approved Transaction in accordance with applicable Thai law.

This Item 7.01 of this Current Report on Form 8-K is being furnished to the Securities and Exchange Commission under Item 7.01 of Form 8-K in satisfaction of the public disclosure requirements of Regulation FD and shall not be deemed "filed" for any purpose.

The full text of this SEC filing can be retrieved at: https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1838406/000110465926007710/tm264307d1_8k.htm

Any exhibits and associated documents for this SEC filing can be retrieved at: https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1838406/000110465926007710/0001104659-26-007710-index.htm

Public companies must file a Form 8-K, or current report, with the SEC generally within four days of any event that could materially affect a company's financial position or the value of its shares.

data.title: Belgium GDP Grows 0.2% In Q4
data.description:
BRUSSELS/FRANKFURT/PARIS (dpa-AFX) - Belgium's economy grew at a slower pace in the final quarter of 2025, preliminary data from the National Bank of Belgium showed on Thursday.

Gross domestic product grew seasonally and calendar-adjusted 0.2 percent in the fourth quarter, after rising 0.3 percent in the third quarter. The economy has been expanding since the fourth quarter of 2023.

Data showed that value added was up 0.4 percent in construction. In the services sector, activity increased by 0.3 percent, while value added in the industrial sector was 0.6 percent lower.

Compared to the same quarter last year, GDP growth improved slightly to 1.1 percent from 1.0 percent in the second quarter.

Copyright(c) 2026 RTTNews.com. All Rights Reserved

Copyright RTT News/dpa-AFX

data.title: Trane Technologies quarterly profit rises on strong commercial air-conditioning demand
data.description:
Trane Technologies posted a rise in fourth-quarter profit and revenue on Thursday, as the rapid expansion of artificial intelligence-focused data centers lifted demand for its commercial air-conditioning systems.

Trane's acquisition of Stellar Energy's digital business and a 49% stake in Kieback & Peter Group during the quarter expanded its presence in the EMEA region and the data-center cooling business.

Trane's adjusted profit rose to $2.86 per share during the quarter ended December 31, from $2.61 a year ago.

The Ireland-based company's revenue rose to $5.15 billion, from $4.87 billion a year ago.

Analysts on average expected fourth-quarter revenue of $5.08 billion, according to data compiled by LSEG.

Nvidia CEO Jensen Huang recently said that his company’s upcoming chips could significantly reduce cooling requirements in data centers. That could have implications for air-conditioning companies such as Trane Technologies, which derives roughly 10% of its sales from data center-related cooling systems.

data.title: Italy to align its levy on small parcels with EU scheme, economy minister says
data.description:
Italy plans to amend its levy on small packages of goods sent from non-EU countries to align it with the higher European Union tax which is set to enter into force in July, Economy Minister Giancarlo Giorgetti said on Thursday.

Both taxes are part of efforts to crack down on cheap Chinese e-commerce imports such as from online retailers such as Shein and Temu .

"We will try to make our tax consistent with decisions at European level," he told reporters on the sidelines of an event in Rome.

This year's budget imposed a tax of 2 euros ($2.39) on parcels valued at up to 150 euros, with the levy expected to generate 122.5 million euros this year and 245 million in both 2027 and 2028.

European Union authorities set a 3 euro customs duty on low-value parcels arriving in the bloc.

The duty will apply per item type from July 1, 2026, and will be in place until a permanent solution is found to eliminate the "de minimis" duties exemption for online purchases below 150 euros.

The co-ruling Forza Italia party is asking the government to freeze the Italian levy until July.

"We will assess" that request, Giorgetti said.

($1 = 0.8368 euros)

data.title: Sibanye Stillwater aims for 50% reduction in gross debt by 2028
data.description:
Sibanye stillwater is targeting a 50% reduction in gross debt in two-to-three years' time from around $2.2 billion now, the company said on Thursday.

The diversified miner is also targeting 3 billion rand ($190.10 million) in cost savings per year by 2027, it said in a strategy update.

($1 = 15.7814 rand)

data.title: Honeywell rises as Q4 profit beats estimates
data.description:
** Shares of industrial conglomerate Honeywell rise 1.2% to $219.15 in choppy premarket trading, after being negative earlier

** Q4 adj. EPS of $2.59 beat analysts' estimates of $2.54, according to data compiled by LSEG

** Q4 sales of $9.8 bln slightly miss analysts' estimates of $9.85 bln- LSEG data

** Results buoyed by strength in aerospace unit and related aftermarket services

** 18 of 30 brokerages rate the stock "buy" or higher, 11 "hold" and one "sell"; their median PT is $235.50- LSEG data

** Up to last close, HON up 11% YTD vs a 8.3% decline in 2025

data.title: VSEC: Acquisition forms a global aviation aftermarket leader, targeting $15M+ synergies and 20%+ margins
data.description:
The acquisition will create a leading, scaled aviation aftermarket platform with expanded global reach, diversified customer base, and enhanced proprietary repair capabilities. The $2.025B deal is expected to deliver over $15M in annualized synergies and drive margin expansion above 20% as integration progresses.

data.title: USHAMART: Quarterly net profit after tax was Rs. 10,767 lakhs, with stable segment performance and legal matters ongoing
data.description:

data.title: Gentherm Inc Announces Combination with Modine Performance Technologies
data.description:
On January 29, 2026, Gentherm Inc announced a definitive agreement to combine with Modine's Performance Technologies business.

Key Highlights:

  • Transaction valued at ~$1 billion, creating a leader in thermal management solutions.
  • Gentherm shareholders to own 60% of the combined company, Modine shareholders 40%.
  • Expected $25 million in annual cost synergies and expanded market opportunities.
  • Combined company to generate pro forma revenue of $2.6 billion with a 13% EBITDA margin.
  • Transaction expected to close in Q4 2026, subject to regulatory approvals.

Original SEC Filing: Gentherm Inc [ THRM ] - 8-K - Jan. 29, 2026

Disclaimer
This is an AI-powered summary. It may contain inaccuracies. Consider verifying important information with the source. Please note this summary is solely based on documents filed with the SEC.

data.title: GCT Semiconductor Announces Licensing Agreement with Satellite Provider
data.description:
On January 29, 2026, GCT Semiconductor Holding Inc announced a licensing agreement with a major satellite communications provider.

Key Highlights:

  • GCT licenses 5G and 4G chipsets to a leading satellite provider.
  • Agreement enables integration into satellite user equipment.
  • Shipments of 5G products to start in the second half of 2026.
  • Collaboration aims to enhance global 5G-to-space networks.
  • Devices to offer improved non-terrestrial connectivity.

Original SEC Filing: GCT Semiconductor Holding, Inc. [ GCTS ] - 8-K - Jan. 29, 2026

data.title: Unicycive rises as FDA accepts resubmitted filing for kidney drug
data.description:
** Shares of drug developer Unicycive Therapeutics rise 9.5% to $7.69 premarket

** Co says FDA accepted for review its resubmitted application for oxylanthanum carbonate, a pill for high phosphate levels in dialysis patients

** High phosphate levels occur in people with late‑stage kidney disease when the body cannot remove excess minerals, per co

**  Co says FDA raised no initial concerns on safety or effectiveness; decision expected by June 27

**  UNCY's drug aims to cut pill burden to help patients stay on treatment; possible launch later this year if approved, co says

** Shares down ~27% in 2025

data.title: CCB: Loan and deposit growth continued, with CCBX and BaaS driving fee income and platform expansion
data.description:
Loans and deposits grew sequentially, with CCBX and BaaS segments driving fee income and platform expansion. Net income and EPS declined due to higher noninterest expenses, but book value and capital ratios improved. Technology investments and new partnerships support future growth.

Original document: Coastal Financial Corporation [CCB] Slides Release — Feb. 3 2026

data.title: US pre-open: Futures edge higher as Fed holds rates, tech earnings roll in
data.description:
As of 1215 GMT, Dow Jones futures were up 0.10%, while S&P 500 and Nasdaq-100 futures had the indices opening 0.20% and 0.26% firmer, respectively.

The Dow closed just 12.19 points higher on Wednesday, doing little to reclaim heavy losses recorded in the previous session.

Tech earnings were in focus prior to the open after social media giant Meta delivered a stronger‑than‑expected fourth quarter performance on Wednesday, on the back of a solid performance by its advertising business, while electric carmaker Tesla posted slightly better‑than‑expected fourth‑quarter results, even as full‑year revenue slipped 3% - the first annual decline on record.

Software giant Microsoft, on the other hand, traded lower in pre-market action despite topping quarterly expectations, after it reported a slight slowdown in cloud growth.

In terms of Thursday's earnings reports, trade bellwether Caterpillar posted increased quarterly profit and revenues, driven by AI-fueled power equipment demand, but flagged a $2.6bn tariff hit for 2026.

Still to come, Mastercard and Lockheed Martin were slated to report before the open, while Apple and Visa will report after the close.

The Federal Reserve was also in focus after the central bakn left interest rates unchanged on Wednesday, holding its benchmark range at 3.5%–3.75% after three cuts late last year, as policymakers waited for clearer evidence on how earlier easing is feeding through to the economy.

Officials have signalled they want more time to assess the impact of those reductions and indicated policy would only be loosened once price pressures begin to ease. "Uncertainty about the economic outlook remains elevated," the Fed said, adding that the committee remained alert to risks on both sides of its mandate.

The decision came as the central bank faces heightened scrutiny over its independence, with the Supreme Court reviewing a case that could have major implications for its authority. Chairman Jerome Powell earlier this month publicly pushed back against pressure from the Trump administration and reiterated a strong defence of Fed independence in his post-meeting presser, describing it as essential to preventing political influence over monetary policy and warning that its erosion would be difficult to reverse.

Spot gold was also drawing an amount of investor attention after surpassing the $5,500 mark in overnight trading, last up 3.76%, as it continued to surge to new records as the US dollar continued to slide. The US dollar index, meanwhile, was down 0.04% at 96.41 early on Thursday after sinking to its lowest level in four years on Wednesday.

Donald Trump played down the recent weakness in the US dollar during a visit to Iowa, telling reporters he viewed the currency's slide positively as he pointed to what he described as strong underlying economic activity.

The softer USD has also helped push several major currencies to multi‑year highs, with the Swiss franc climbing to its strongest level against the greenback in more than a decade, while the euro also strengthened, rising to $1.20 and marking its biggest weekly advance since last spring.

On Thursday's macro slate, weekly jobless claims data from the Labor Department will be published at 1330 GMT, as will November wholesale inventories figures, Q3 nonfarm productivity numbers, and November's goods and services trade balance report, while November factory orders data will follow at 1500 GMT.

Reporting by Iain Gilbert at Sharecast.com

data.title: US Futures Edge Up, Earnings Eyed
data.description:
US stock futures edged slightly higher on Thursday, with contracts on the three major indices up nearly 0.1%, as investors digested a fresh round of corporate earnings, particularly from the technology sector.

Meta shares surged more than 8% in premarket trading after the company’s sales outlook beat expectations.

Tesla gained 2.7% following an earnings beat, despite reporting a 3% decline in annual revenue, the first such drop in the company’s history.

IBM jumped nearly 10% and Caterpillar rose 1.3% after both companies posted results that topped estimates.

Honeywell also edged higher, up 1.2%, despite reporting mixed results.

Apple added 0.7% ahead of its quarterly earnings release later today after the closing bell.

In contrast, Microsoft shares fell 7.5% after the company reported slowing cloud growth.
